segments.text: Day two of rebuilding my creator
business until I can work with 100
people. And I'm going to show you how
exactly I plan to lock the in and
actually make it feel easy. And we're
not using discipline, motivation, or
just willpower to muscle our way through
to do things that we don't feel like
doing. What you actually need is an
identity change. We're going to reframe
your perspective. A bodybuilder doesn't
struggle to go to the gym. Why? Because
they're a bodybuilder. That's their
identity. That's what they like to do.
That's the lifestyle that they have
taught their brain to enjoy. It's not
what they do, it's who they are and they
maintain their identity. Therefore, it's
painful and difficult for a bodybuilder
to actually take a rest day and stay at
home in bed all day. Your brain is a
goal achieving machine. It's always
working towards something whether you
know it or not. The problem is most of
your goals were programmed by your
environment and your brain's number one
subconscious goal is to always remain
the same to maintain status quo to
maintain homeostasis. So the question
isn't how do you become more
disciplined. The question is who do you
need to become? What identity shift do
you need to make to then make the hard
things that you want to be doing easy?
And the shift is through small
consistent actions to then alter your
identity. Plus, with some mindfulness
exercises like journaling or meditation,
you should be able to do this in just a
few days. Every time you do the thing,
you're casting a vote to what your
identity is and who you are becoming. Do
it enough times and your brain's going
to rewire itself. You don't build
discipline, you just redirect the
discipline that you already have. Today,
I just filmed a full YouTube video on
this that's going to be out in a couple
days. Comment YT or click the link in my
bio and you can check it out. Okay.
Peace.
